summary.name: Monthly Cross-Asset Relative Strength Min-Variance Allocator
summary.author: Matthew Wang
summary.description: The strategy is a monthly cross asset ETF allocator that seeks to capture medium term relative strength while controlling risk via minimum variance weighting. Each rebalance it ranks a predefined universe of liquid US listed ETFs spanning US equities (VTI, QQQ), developed ex US equities (VXUS, EFA), real estate (VNQ), core bonds and duration exposures (BND, IEF, TLT), and gold (GLD) by their trailing 180 day cumulative log return, selects the top four performers, and treats this as the opportuni
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a monthly cross asset ETF allocator that seeks to capture medium term relative strength while controlling risk via minimum variance weighting. Each rebalance it ranks a predefined universe of liquid US listed ETFs spanning US equities (VTI, QQQ), developed ex US equities (VXUS, EFA), real estate (VNQ), core bonds and duration exposures (BND, IEF, TLT), and gold (GLD) by their trailing 180 day cumulative log return, selects the top four performers, and treats this as the opportunity set in which trend and persistence are most likely to be rewarded.

For the selected four, it estimates a covariance matrix using the most recent 20 trading days of log returns and runs a Monte Carlo search over random fully invested long only weight vectors to find the allocation with the lowest annualized volatility. It then immediately rebalances to those weights and holds until the next month start rebalance, with decisions made on daily close data and executed near the close (about 5 minutes before) on the scheduled rebalance day. Risk control is implemented through diversification across the top four assets and explicit minimization of forecast volatility; there are no explicit stop losses, leverage, cash targeting, or drawdown constraints beyond the optimizer’s long only fully invested allocation.

raw.name: Monthly Cross-Asset Relative Strength Min-Variance Allocator
raw.description:
The strategy is a monthly cross asset ETF allocator that seeks to capture medium term relative strength while controlling risk via minimum variance weighting. Each rebalance it ranks a predefined universe of liquid US listed ETFs spanning US equities (VTI, QQQ), developed ex US equities (VXUS, EFA), real estate (VNQ), core bonds and duration exposures (BND, IEF, TLT), and gold (GLD) by their trailing 180 day cumulative log return, selects the top four performers, and treats this as the opportunity set in which trend and persistence are most likely to be rewarded.

For the selected four, it estimates a covariance matrix using the most recent 20 trading days of log returns and runs a Monte Carlo search over random fully invested long only weight vectors to find the allocation with the lowest annualized volatility. It then immediately rebalances to those weights and holds until the next month start rebalance, with decisions made on daily close data and executed near the close (about 5 minutes before) on the scheduled rebalance day. Risk control is implemented through diversification across the top four assets and explicit minimization of forecast volatility; there are no explicit stop losses, leverage, cash targeting, or drawdown constraints beyond the optimizer’s long only fully invested allocation.
